title: Health check endpoint (GET /health)

story.i_want_to: implement a GET /health endpoint that returns the application health status including database connectivity, Oban status, and application version
story.so_that: monitoring systems, load balancers, and deployment scripts can verify the application is running and its dependencies are healthy

rationale: Health checks are essential for production operations. The beelink deployment script needs to verify the app started successfully after deploys. Nginx upstream health checks prevent routing to a dead backend. Monitoring systems use health endpoints to detect outages. This endpoint must be unauthenticated so external probes can reach it.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /health returns 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok", "version": "<app_version>", "checks": {...}} when all checks pass
The endpoint is unauthenticated — no API key required
The checks object includes "database": "ok" when a simple SELECT 1 query succeeds against PostgreSQL
The checks object includes "oban": "ok" when Oban is running in the supervision tree
If the database check fails, the endpoint returns 503 with {"status": "degraded", "checks": {"database": "error", ...}}
The version field is read from the application spec (:loopctl, :vsn) or mix.exs project version
The endpoint responds within 5 seconds even if a dependency check is slow (timeout handling)

test_cases.name: Health check returns 200 when all systems are healthy
expected_results: Response body has a non-empty version string

test_cases.name: Health check does not require authentication
steps: Send GET /health without any Authorization header

test_cases.name: Health check returns 503 when database is unavailable
steps: Mock the database check to return {:error, :timeout}

test_cases.name: Health check includes application version
steps: Parse the version field from the response
expected_results: Version field is a non-empty string matching semver pattern or mix project version

test_cases.name: Health check responds with correct JSON content type

technical_notes: Route should be outside the /api/v1 scope and auth pipeline: get "/health", HealthController, :check in the top-level scope
Database check: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query(Repo, "SELECT 1") wrapped in a try/catch or Task.await with timeout
Use a dedicated Loopctl.Health module for the check logic, keep the controller thin
Consider adding a response_time_ms field for each check for observability
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
Database health check MUST be injectable via behaviour-based DI for test mocking. Define a HealthCheck behaviour, mock it in test/support/mocks.ex.
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
